input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule F.4.1 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: f.4.1
            TITLE: requirements

            CONTENT:
            rule F.4.1: Requirements
F.4.1 Requirements 
If any composite or other material is used, the SES must contain:

RULE: f.4.1.3
            TITLE: calculations that show equivalence of the structure to one of similar geometry made to meet

            CONTENT:
            rule F.4.1.3: Calculations that show equivalence of the structure to one of similar geometry made to meet
F.4.1.3 Calculations that show equivalence of the structure to one of similar geometry made to meet 
the minimum requirements for a structure made from steel tubing per F.3.2. Equivalency 
calculations must be submitted for energy dissipation, yield and ultimate strengths in bending, 
buckling, and tension.

RULE: f.4.1.1
            TITLE: documentation of material type, (purchase receipt, shipping document or letter of donation)

            CONTENT:
            rule F.4.1.1: Documentation of material type, (purchase receipt, shipping document or letter of donation)
F.4.1.1 Documentation of material type, (purchase receipt, shipping document or letter of donation) 
and the material properties.

RULE: f.4.1.2
            TITLE: details of the manufacturing technique and/or composite layup technique as well as the

            CONTENT:
            rule F.4.1.2: Details of the manufacturing technique and/or composite layup technique as well as the
F.4.1.2 Details of the manufacturing technique and/or composite layup technique as well as the 
structural material used (examples - cloth type, weight, and resin type, number of layers, core 
material, and skin material if metal).

RULE: f.4.1.4
            TITLE: construction dates of the test panel(s) and monocoque, and approximate age(s) of the

            CONTENT:
            rule F.4.1.4: Construction dates of the test panel(s) and monocoque, and approximate age(s) of the
F.4.1.4 Construction dates of the test panel(s) and monocoque, and approximate age(s) of the 
materials used. 
The intent is for the test panel to use the same material batch, material age, material storage, 
and student layup quality as the monocoque.

final_answer: rule F.4.1: Requirements F.4.1 Requirements If any composite or other material is used, the SES must contain:
